channel.title: Philosophy of Mathematics on Marginalia
channel.description: Recent content in Philosophy of Mathematics on Marginalia

item.title: Imagining the Imaginary: A Cultural-Intellectual History of the Complex Plane (1748--2025)
item.description: Imagining the Imaginary: A Cultural-Intellectual History of the Complex Plane (1748–2025)

item.title: The Semantics of "Geometry" from Euler to Today (Europe & the United States)
item.description: Abstract: This report traces the evolving meanings of “geometry” in European and American mathematics from the era of Leonhard Euler (18th century) to the present day. Over nearly three centuries, “geometry” has expanded from denoting the classical study of shapes and Euclidean space to a sprawling family of subfields and methodologies. We examine what mathematicians of each era understood “geometry” to mean, how new theories and external pressures reshaped those meanings, and how “geometry” functioned both as a subject area and a style of reasoning. Key transitions include the rise of analytic and coordinate methods, the introduction of projective and non-Euclidean geometries, the 19th-century unification of geometry via transformation groups (Klein’s Erlangen Program), the axiomatizatio…

item.title: The Tragedy of Formalism
item.description: Formalism as tragedy, computation as poetry: from Hilbert and intuitionism to a proposed algebra of iterated operations on polynomials via syntactic encoding.

item.title: Truth as Process
item.description: An essay arguing that the halting problem’s paradox is partly a self-imposed constraint: demanding a point answer to a line-shaped process. It proposes treating truth as something that can be conferred by an evolving process rather than a single atomic verdict.
